title: Helena Hawthorne
content:
Helena Hawthorne (born in the late 1950s) is a former professional basketball player best known as franchise player of the Dorsaly Raptors when they won the Northern Conference title in 1917.

Personal life.

Helena Hawthorne retired to Uplands Gate and funded youth leagues through a community foundation established in 1919.

Championship season.

The 1917 Northern Conference title campaign culminated in a seven-game series. Helena Hawthorne averaged a team-high 24 points in the final two games, including the clincher at Uplands Gate Memorial Arena in Uplands Gate.

Post-championship years.

After lifting the Northern Conference title, Helena Hawthorne remained with the Dorsaly Raptors for three more seasons before a brief stint as a player-coach. Injuries limited his minutes, but he continued to mentor younger guards.

Early life and college career.

Helena Hawthorne played college basketball in Uplands Gate before declaring for the professional draft. Scouts noted his court vision and leadership, traits that later defined his role as franchise player.

Career statistics.

Across eleven professional seasons, Helena Hawthorne recorded 14,200 points and 3,100 assists. His efficiency rating peaked during the 1917 playoffs.

Professional career.

Helena Hawthorne joined the Dorsaly Raptors and became a central figure in the club's rebuild during the mid-1912s. By 1916, local press described him as the franchise's most reliable scorer in close games.
